question.text: Vikram (age 35) earns Rs. 12,00,000 per annum. He has 25 years of working life remaining. Using the Human Life Value (HLV) method with inflation at 6% and expected investment return of 10%, what is the additional life insurance cover Vikram needs if he already has a term plan of Rs. 50 lakh?

answer.text: Rs. 1.92 crore (no deduction needed as HLV exceeds existing cover)

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Step 1: Real (inflation-adjusted) discount rate = ((1.10)/(1.06)) - 1 = 3.7736%. Step 2: PV of annuity factor for 25 years at 3.7736% = (1 - (1.037736)^(-25)) / 0.037736 = 16.0028. Step 3: HLV = Rs. 12,00,000 x 16.0028 = Rs. 1,92,03,305 ~ Rs. 1.92 crore. Step 4: Additional cover needed = Rs. 1.92 crore - Rs. 0.50 crore = Rs. 1.42 crore. **The Trap:** Rs. 1.92 crore is the full HLV without deducting the existing Rs. 50 lakh cover — candidates must remember to subtract existing insurance. Rs. 1.70 crore incorrectly uses nominal 10% rate without inflation adjustment. **Cross-Exam Context:** NISM Series X-A covers insurance planning basics. The HLV method is also tested in IRDA agent exams. NISM X-B specifically expects candidates to calculate insurance gaps. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Always account for existing group insurance from employers, personal term plans, and any endowment/ULIP cover when computing the gap. Most Indians are underinsured — the average cover is barely 10x annual income when 15-20x is often needed.
